originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and curves scripts, then initializes many inline control-point, interpolation, spline, patch, and subdivision-surface sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the drag, interpolation, spline, and subdivision scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with draggable control-point curves and then steps through linear-segment and interpolation examples with slider-driven progress and synchronized weighting views.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening control-point scenes and the early interpolation examples under the original published runtime.
notes: The smoke contract treats one control-point drag path and one interpolation slider path as required parity behavior.
The route preserves the published progression from freeform control points into linear interpolation instead of flattening those sections into static diagrams.
evidence: Post-airfoil rollout planning called for one control-point drag, one progress slider, and responsive-shell validation.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a control-point scene responds to drag and a linear-segment scene updates from slider input.

originalBehavior: The published article continues through Bézier patches, tangents, splines, Chaikin subdivision, topological curve subdivision, and Catmull-Clark-style surface subdivision scenes with linked sliders and canvas redraws.
localStatus: The local route keeps the spline, tangent, and later subdivision-surface scenes intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The shipped `gears` tangency reference is rewritten locally in the public body.
This route remains self-contained without a separate route-local model or texture bundle.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one spline or subdivision slider scene and one later surface subdivision scene as parity-critical for the batch.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a later curve-subdivision scene and a later surface-subdivision scene update from slider input without runtime asset failures.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with further-reading sections and an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance, while also referring back to other Bartosz articles by upstream URL.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published closing sections while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and rewriting available local cross-links.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The public body no longer includes the Patreon footer prompt or other author-promo surfaces.
evidence: The hardened Ciechanowski audit flags ordinary in-body upstream article links while allowing footer-only provenance.
Route cleanup rewrote the local `gears` tangency reference so the family continues converging toward local intra-family navigation.
